call_meta.persona: American expat in Maastricht, accompanying her partner (a master's student). Belonging + Integration + Career motivations. Barriers: Production gap, Access to practice, Prior course skepticism, Shame. Not on the inburgering track -- her decision to stay in the Netherlands hinges entirely on whether she can find a path to actually speak Dutch. Warm Dutchwithjoost pilot prospect.
call_meta.industry: Not currently working; aspiring founder of a coaching business for autistic people

jtbd.job: Decide whether to stay in the Netherlands or move to Ireland
jtbd.desired_outcome: A credible path to speak Dutch that makes staying feasible; language is the single deciding factor
jtbd.current_approach: Undecided and leaning toward Ireland because Dutch feels like a dead end for jobs and connection

jtbd.job: Learn enough Dutch to get by in daily life
jtbd.desired_outcome: Not having to switch to English in everyday situations like grocery stores; being polite by at least starting in Dutch
jtbd.current_approach: Two-week intensive in-person course (just started); Duolingo before that

jtbd.job: Connect with Dutch people and stop feeling excluded
jtbd.desired_outcome: Make Dutch friends, follow conversations, feel part of the community rather than only in the expat bubble
jtbd.current_approach: Joined volunteering groups but felt excluded when others spoke Dutch; failed to make Dutch friends

jtbd.job: Build a viable future (a coaching business) in the Netherlands
jtbd.desired_outcome: Job and business prospects that aren't blocked by the language barrier
jtbd.current_approach: Believes the business has no future here without Dutch

pains.pain: The language barrier is the sole reason she may leave the country
pains.trigger: Failed social connection and fear of no job prospects without Dutch
pains.cost: Strongly considering emigrating to Ireland; would settle in the Netherlands if she could speak Dutch
pains.verbatim: The pain is so big that that is the only reason why they want to move to an english speaking country.

pains.pain: Exclusion and failure to make Dutch friends
pains.trigger: Volunteering groups dominated by Dutch speakers who talked among themselves
pains.cost: Isolation; ended up only socializing with other expats

pains.pain: Guilt and disconnection when Dutch people switch to English for her
pains.cost: Feels bad making people switch; feels disconnected when she can't follow what people say

pains.pain: Can't ask questions in a large class, so she doesn't actually understand
pains.trigger: Group of 12 in the intensive course; teacher moves through grammar and verb conjugation
pains.cost: Often doesn't understand the phrases or translations, feels bad asking, so she stays lost

pains.pain: Can't retain vocabulary and verb conjugations from the course
pains.trigger: Book-based intensive format with heavy memorization
pains.cost: Struggles to keep words and conjugations in mind; wishes she had a cheatsheet of words and meanings

pains.pain: Self-blame -- assumes she is just bad at it
pains.cost: Internalizes the failure rather than blaming the method

pains.trigger: Three hours of class plus heavy homework
pains.cost: Won't take an intensive course again for the next levels

workflow_reality.current_process: Enrolled in a two-week intensive in-person course (A0 to A1, four days a week, three hours daily, group of 12): read a dialogue from the book, listen to it, then the teacher works through grammar and verb conjugation, followed by substantial homework. Previously tried Duolingo. Now evaluating Dutchwithjoost.
workflow_reality.brittleness: Can't ask questions in a group of 12; translations and phrases go unexplained; heavy homework; content doesn't stick
workarounds: Chose the course because her boyfriend had taken it before her

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Two-week intensive in-person course (A0 to A1)
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Currently enrolled; group of 12 too large to ask questions, content doesn't stick, intensive format overwhelming -- but it is what she's doing right now

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Duolingo
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Tried it a couple of times; didn't give phrases usable in everyday life

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Dutchwithjoost
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Shown the product and liked it; initial worry about grammar coverage was resolved by seeing the grammar feedback; asked to try the first lesson free but hesitant to commit while in her current course and undecided about staying

quotes.text: So that I don't have to switch to English in grocery stores.
quotes.speaker: prospect -- American expat
quotes.context: Describing her near-term goal for learning Dutch

quotes.text: Sorry my Dutch is not good, can we do English?
quotes.speaker: prospect -- American expat
quotes.context: The minimum she wants to be able to say, to feel she is being polite

quotes.text: It doesn't have to be face to face, I don't need that... but it would be nice if someone is taking the course with me, that I can talk to them, that would keep me away from giving up when it's too difficult.
quotes.speaker: prospect -- American expat
quotes.context: Answering how important face-to-face or an accountability buddy is

quotes.text: If she spoke Dutch fluently, would she consider staying? Yes -- we like everything else and would actually settle here.
quotes.speaker: interviewer (paraphrase of prospect)
quotes.context: Establishing that language is the single deciding factor in the stay-or-leave decision

deviant_terms: cheatsheet of the words and their meaning
don't have to switch to English in grocery stores

keyword_candidates.source_context: Currently in a two-week intensive and comparing it to Dutchwithjoost after seeing a demo

keyword_candidates.keyword: learn enough Dutch to get by in daily life
keyword_candidates.source_context: Wants to stop switching to English in grocery stores and everyday situations

keyword_candidates.keyword: intensive Dutch course too overwhelming can't keep up
keyword_candidates.source_context: Three hours plus heavy homework; can't retain vocabulary and conjugations

keyword_candidates.keyword: Dutch course small enough to ask questions
keyword_candidates.source_context: Group of 12 too large; she feels bad asking the teacher to translate

keyword_candidates.keyword: how to make Dutch friends as an expat
keyword_candidates.source_context: Excluded in volunteering groups; failed to make Dutch friends

keyword_candidates.keyword: should I leave the Netherlands if I can't learn Dutch
keyword_candidates.source_context: Language is the single reason she and her partner are considering Ireland
